untranslatable_reason: This signal requires pair trading mechanics with cross-instrument correlation analysis, rolling spread calculations between two correlated instruments, and dynamic standardized spread thresholds. The allowed indicators do not support: (1) calculating spreads between two different instruments, (2) rolling correlation measurement between instrument pairs, (3) rolling median spread calculation, (4) standardized deviation of spread from rolling median, or (5) directional logic dependent on which leg is overpriced vs underpriced. This is a multivariate statistical arbitrage signal that requires custom pair-trading infrastructure not representable in single-instrument technical indicator rules.
translation_notes: MICROSTRUCTURE_DRY_17_2 is a pairs trading / statistical arbitrage strategy requiring dual-instrument analysis. The core logic involves: (1) identifying correlated pairs with correlation > 0.70, (2) calculating 63-day rolling median spread, (3) measuring current spread deviation in standard deviations, (4) entering when divergence exceeds 1.0 std dev with equal notional exposure on both legs, and (5) exiting on convergence to 0.25 std dev or stop-loss at 2.0 std dev. This cannot be expressed as conditions on single-instrument price/technical indicators and requires a dedicated pairs trading engine.
